spec_match.rationale: The demo now places a form-associated custom element inside a real form, registers `attachInternals({ behaviors: [new HTMLSubmitButtonBehavior()] })` when available, and otherwise falls back to the same form submission path with the unsupported state visible.

interactive.rationale: DevTools exercised real insert, form fill, custom submit, move-outside, disabled-fieldset, reset, and removal controls with live status and FormData updates.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature-index blurb promises form lifecycle callbacks and reflected form state, and the page now logs association, disassociation, disabled, reset, and submit behavior in the actual form.

edge_cases.rationale: The page covers unsupported `HTMLSubmitButtonBehavior`, no-form activation, fieldset-disabled suppression, reset cleanup, and reinsertion after moving the element outside the form.

design_system.rationale: The controls use shared CSS variables, paired background/color button overrides, focus-visible styling, and a live UI rather than a static code card.

summary: Verified with chrome-devtools-mcp on http://localhost:3000/v149/platform-provided-behaviors/form-lifecycle-demo/ in Chrome 148. The browser does not expose HTMLSubmitButtonBehavior, so the unsupported banner appeared. DevTools filled the username/email fields, inserted the custom element into the real form, clicked the custom submitter, used the action submit button, moved the element outside the form, clicked the no-form branch, reinserted it, disabled the fieldset, attempted submission while disabled, reset the form, and removed the element. Observed states changed from associated/form#demoForm/in form.elements to null outside the form, disabled changed to YES while the fieldset was disabled, submit count stayed unchanged during the disabled attempt, FormData showed the visible field values, console had no errors, and network requests were expected 200/204 responses.
